verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonical cytosolic heat shock protein (Hsp70 family member). It functions as a molecular chaperone stabilizing and folding proteins in the cytosol and organelles, and participates in the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway. No transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or documented surface localization. Not a secreted protein. The only potential "surface" engagement would be MHC presentation of HSPA1A-derived peptides by intracellular antigen-presentation pathways, which does not constitute surface accessibility of the protein body itself.
